design_philosophy.core_loop: 1v1 competitive fighting with emphasis on special move execution, juggling combos, and offensive pressure through block chip damage
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Learn opponent movesets and frame data vs. execute precise inputs under pressure; mastering special move inputs vs. risk of punishment
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master martial artist executing devastating special moves and finishing moves in tournament combat; earn respect through competitive skill
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Pioneering use of digitized graphics for violence; non-circular joystick inputs for special moves (more accessible than Street Fighter); block chip damage encourages aggressive play; Fatality system as narrative punishment/humiliation mechanic; 5-button layout creates distinct identity from other fighting games

difficulty.continues_system: Single-player tournament mode continues implicitly through character progression; player loses tournament run upon defeat
difficulty.checkpointing: Linear tournament progression with no mid-tournament checkpoints; mirror match, then 3 endurance matches, then sub-boss Goro, then final boss Shang Tsung
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character select provides some agency (different movesets/spacing); no explicit difficulty selector documented; single-player tournament is fixed difficulty ramp

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament progression structure: 7 scripted 1v1 matches → mirror match → 3 endurance matches (health does not regenerate between opponents) → Goro fight → Shang Tsung final boss
replayability_hooks: Seven different playable characters with distinct movesets encourage multiple playthroughs
Test Your Might minigame with escalating difficulty (wood → stone → steel → ruby → diamond) provides score chase
Two-player versus mode offers indefinite competitive replayability

world_building_techniques: Opening cinematic explains Shang Tsung's 500-year banishment and tournament seizing plot
Tournament structure itself (fighting on Shang Tsung's Island across seven stage locations) reinforces world setting
Character select screen and stage variety imply martial arts tournament culture

boss_design.philosophy: Goro functions as sub-boss test of learned tournament skills; Shang Tsung as final narrative antagonist and ultimate skill check. Both are characters within the tournament structure rather than external threats.
notable_bosses: Goro (sub-boss): Undefeated for 500 years, monstrous design, narrative gatekeeper before final confrontation
Shang Tsung (final boss): Tournament architect and primary antagonist, must be defeated to save Earthrealm

faction_indicators: Earthrealm vs. Outworld (implied): Players protecting Earth through tournament victory
Shang Tsung's corrupted tournament vs. legitimate martial arts tradition
danger_signals: Shang Tsung's 500-year domination of tournament signals institutional corruption
Goro's 500-year undefeated streak represents systemic imbalance and threat to realm
Tournament seizing indicates hostile takeover of traditional competition structure
environmental_storytelling: Seven stage locations on Shang Tsung's Island create varied martial arts tournament aesthetic
Character roster composition (martial artists from different disciplines) implies global tournament draw
Island setting isolates competitors and emphasizes tournament as contained conflict zone

sources_used: Wikipedia: Mortal Kombat (1992 video game) - Gameplay section, Plot section, general game specifications
